---
title: "Adding A Record To A Legal Hold Case"
---

When you add a record to a legal hold case, its status changes to On Hold. A record with any status (Incomplete, Ready, Under Retention, or Reached Disposition) is eligible to be stored in a legal hold case. When a record is added to a legal hold case, it continues to follow the rules set when creating the retention policy, which means that the original status is kept and updated in the repository. For example, if a record with status Ready is put on hold, its status changes to On Hold but the original status of Ready is maintained in the repository. When the record reaches the cutoff date the status of this record changes to Under Retention and keeps undergoing changes in accordance with its regular lifecycle. Even if the record reaches its disposition, it cannot be removed while it remains on hold.

You can add a single record to multiple legal hold cases. To add a record to a legal hold case:
1.  Filter your records in Workspace. For more information on filtering records, see Filtering Records.
2.  Select a single record or multiple records that you want to add to a legal hold case.
3.  Click the Add selected records to a legal hold button on the upper right.   ![Add selected records to a legal hold button](data:image/png;base64,)   A Legal Hold Cases window with existing legal hold cases is displayed.
4.  Select a single or multiple Legal Hold Case where you want to add your records. If the Legal Hold Case you want to use does not exist, you can create a new legal hold case. To create a legal hold case, see Creating a Legal Hold Case.
5.  Click the Add Records to Selection button. A message confirming adding records to legal cases is displayed. The status of the selected records change to On Hold.
